verdict.answer_first: A gets to a plan faster: "I'll lay out a plan" then concrete phases; B opens with process baggage: "recall lookup...got blocked" before the answer.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits slightly better to one default stack: "Recommend a dedicated #alerts Slack channel" and "Managed SaaS is the default"; B keeps more forked choices like "Healthchecks.io or Better Stack" and asks to choose surfaces.
verdict.fabrication: Both invent context; A labels it as assumptions: "Aurora PostgreSQL + Supabase...long-running batch". B is worse because it states specifics as fact: "prod 사주/천기문 web app", "PM2/launchd bridge", "evidence-verifier agent".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it maps gaps to tools and gives sequenced phases with concrete URLs, endpoints, alarms, and alert routing; B is useful but more cluttered by unavailable-session explanation and extra inferred infra.
